name: Golden Roast Pheasant
recipeInstructions:
1 Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2 Squeeze the juice of half the orange into the cavity and over the skin of the bird. Rub the inside and outside of the bird with a blend of the remaining ingredients (salt, paprika, parsley, bacon, dried sage, garlic).
3 Place the remaining half of the orange, cut into quarters, in the cavity with the garlic and parsley.
4 Spread butter over the breast of the bird and place it, breast side up, in a shallow pan. Cover with bacon.
5 Cover with foil and bake for 45 minutes.
6 Heat the wine to boiling over high heat. Add raisins and remove from heat. Let stand for 45 minutes.
7 Remove the foil and pour the wine mixture over the pheasant. Bake uncovered, basting frequently, until the juice runs clear when a thigh is pierced (about 45 minutes).
8 Remove the pheasant with bacon to a platter. Spoon some raisin sauce over the top and pour the remainder into a sauceboat. Serve immediately.
